summary: Claude Code CLI Tool Implementation Guide

message.content: O "code", dentro do app claude tem as mesmas capacidades do claude code via CLI?

content.thinking:

content.text: Essa é uma boa pergunta sobre as capacidades do Claude Code. Deixa eu consultar a documentação oficial para te dar uma resposta precisa.

input.prompt:
The user is asking whether "code" within the Claude app (claude.ai web/desktop app) has the same capabilities as Claude Code via CLI. Please explain the differences and similarities between:
1. Claude Code CLI (the terminal-based tool)
2. Any "code" features within the Claude web/desktop app (claude.ai)

Focus on what capabilities each has, what tools/features are available in each, and the key differences.
input.description: Compare Claude Code CLI vs app

content.text:
Based on the official documentation, I can now provide you with a comprehensive comparison between Claude Code CLI and the code features in the Claude web/desktop app.

## Claude Code CLI vs Claude Web/Desktop App - Key Differences

### Claude Code CLI (Terminal-Based Tool)

**Claude Code CLI** is Anthropic's agentic coding tool that runs in your terminal. Key capabilities include:

**Core Capabilities:**
- Edit files directly and execute bash commands
- Run code and tests in your local environment
- Create commits and push to Git repositories
- Full access to your local filesystem and environment
- Works with any code repository on your machine
- Can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)

**Tools & Features:**
- Direct file editing and bash command execution
- Git integration (clone, commit, push, create PRs)
- Extended thinking and Plan Mode for complex tasks
- Custom slash commands and Agent Skills
- Subagent delegation for specialized tasks
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server integration for extensibility
- IDE integrations (VS Code extension, JetBrains plugins)
- Keyboard shortcuts and Vim mode support
- Hooks system (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest, etc.)

**Environment Control:**
- Runs on your local machine with your exact setup
- Access to all your installed tools, languages, and dependencies
- Full control over permissions and security settings
- Can read/write to any accessible directory

---

### Claude Code on the Web/Desktop App (claude.ai)

**Claude Code on the web** is available at [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code) and on the Claude iOS app. It's currently in research preview. Key capabilities include:

**Core Capabilities:**
- Launch Claude Code tasks asynchronously from the web interface
- Works exclusively with GitHub-hosted repositories
- Clone repos to Anthropic-managed virtual machines
- Execute tasks remotely without needing local setup
- Push results to a branch and create PRs directly
- Monitor task progress and steer the agent via web UI
- Move sessions from web back to CLI terminal for continuation

**Tools & Features:**
- Same core code execution tools as CLI (bash, file editing, Git)
- Pre-installed language ecosystems (Python, Node.js, Ruby, PHP, Java, Go, Rust, C++)
- Databases (PostgreSQL, Redis)
- Package managers for all major languages
- Network access controls (limited by default with allowlisted domains)
- SessionStart hooks for environment configuration
- Environment variables support

**Environment Control:**
- Runs in isolated, Anthropic-managed virtual machines
- Pre-built universal image with common toolchains
- Configurable network access (limited, disabled, or custom)
- Limited to default allowed domains for security
- GitHub operations via secure proxy with scoped credentials

---

## Detailed Comparison Table

| Feature | Claude Code CLI | Claude Code on Web |
|---------|-----------------|-------------------|
| **Execution Location** | Local machine | Anthropic cloud VMs |
| **Repository Access** | Any local repo | GitHub only |
| **Authentication** | Local tools & credentials | GitHub + proxy auth |
| **Environment Setup** | Your exact local setup | Pre-built universal image |
| **Tools Access** | All local tools | Pre-installed languages only |
| **File System** | Full access to local paths | Isolated to repo clone |
| **Network Access** | Depends on local network | Limited by default, configurable |
| **IDE Integration** | VS Code extension, JetBrains plugins | Web UI + can open in CLI |
| **Custom Tools** | MCP servers, scripts, hooks | hooks via SessionStart |
| **Availability** | All users with Claude API access | Pro/Max/Team premium/Enterprise only |
| **Status** | Production ready | Research preview |
| **Git Operations** | Full Git access | GitHub proxy (limited scope) |
| **Running Multiple Tasks** | Sequential or with worktrees | Parallel execution supported |
| **Best For** | Local development, CI integration | Quick tasks, remote repos, parallel work |

---

## Key Similarities

Both versions:
- Use the same underlying Claude Code technology
- Support bash command execution and file editing
- Have Git integration for commits and PRs
- Support hooks for automation
- Can be steered interactively
- Understand your codebase context
- Support extended thinking and analysis

---

## Important Notes

1. **Moving Between Environments**: You can start a task on the web, then click "Open in CLI" to continue locally in your terminal.

2. **Limitations**: Claude Code on the web only works with GitHub repositories. If you use GitLab or other platforms, you must use the CLI.

3. **Permissions**: The web version runs with security restrictions (isolated VMs, limited network access by default), while the CLI runs with your local environment's permissions.

4. **Use Cases**:
   - **Use CLI**: Local development, full tool control, CI/CD automation, non-GitHub repos
   - **Use Web**: Remote tasks, parallel work, monitoring on-the-go, GitHub-only repos

For official details, refer to:
- [Claude Code CLI Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview.md)
- [Claude Code on the Web Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web.md)

content.thinking: The agent provided a comprehensive comparison. Let me summarize the key points for the user in Portuguese.

content.text:
## Resumo: Claude Code CLI vs Claude Code na Web

**Não são exatamente iguais.** Aqui estão as principais diferenças:

### Claude Code CLI (Terminal)
- Roda **localmente** na sua máquina
- Acesso a **qualquer repositório** (Git, GitLab, local, etc.)
- Usa **todas as ferramentas** instaladas no seu ambiente
- Acesso completo ao sistema de arquivos
- Integração com IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains)
- Suporte a MCP servers personalizados

### Claude Code na Web (claude.ai/code)
- Roda em **VMs gerenciadas pela Anthropic** (na nuvem)
- Funciona **apenas com GitHub**
- Ambiente pré-configurado (Python, Node, Go, Rust, etc.)
- Acesso de rede limitado por padrão
- Permite executar tarefas em **paralelo**
- Status: **research preview** (ainda em teste)

### Pontos em comum
- Mesma tecnologia base
- Edição de arquivos e execução de comandos bash
- Integração com Git (commits, PRs)
- Extended thinking para tarefas complexas

### Quando usar cada um?
| Situação | Usar |
|----------|------|
| Desenvolvimento local | CLI |
| Repositórios não-GitHub | CLI |
| Ferramentas/configs específicas | CLI |
| Tarefas rápidas remotas | Web |
| Executar múltiplas tarefas em paralelo | Web |
| Monitorar progresso pelo celular | Web |

Você pode até começar na web e depois clicar "Open in CLI" para continuar localmente.

message.content: Me refiro ao app para macos da anthropic

content.thinking:
The user is clarifying they're asking about the Claude desktop app for macOS (the Anthropic desktop application), not the web version or CLI. They want to know if the "code" features in the macOS Claude app have the same capabilities as Claude Code CLI.

Let me think about this - the Claude desktop app for macOS is essentially a wrapper around the claude.ai web interface, with some additional features like MCP server support. It's not the same as Claude Code CLI which is a terminal-based tool.

I should clarify what the Claude macOS desktop app offers versus Claude Code CLI.

content.text:
Ah, entendi! Você está perguntando sobre o **Claude Desktop App para macOS** (o app nativo da Anthropic).

## Claude Desktop App ≠ Claude Code CLI

São ferramentas **diferentes**:

### Claude Desktop App (macOS)
- É basicamente uma versão desktop do **claude.ai**
- Interface de chat conversacional
- Suporta **MCP servers** para extensibilidade
- Pode analisar arquivos que você anexa
- **Não tem** acesso direto ao seu terminal/filesystem
- **Não executa** comandos bash no seu sistema
- **Não edita** arquivos diretamente no seu projeto

### Claude Code CLI
- Roda no **terminal**
- Acesso **completo** ao filesystem
- **Executa** comandos bash
- **Edita** arquivos diretamente
- Cria commits, PRs, roda testes
- Ferramenta de coding agentic completa

## Em resumo

O app macOS é um **chat inteligente** com suporte a MCP, mas **não é uma ferramenta de coding agentic** como o Claude Code CLI.

Para ter as capacidades completas de edição de código, execução de comandos e integração com seu ambiente de desenvolvimento, você precisa usar o **Claude Code CLI** no terminal:

```bash
# Instalar
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# Usar
claude
```

Faz sentido a diferença?
